title: Using Immersive Reader for reading support
what_it_is: Immersive Reader is a free, built-in Microsoft tool that reads text aloud and simplifies how a page looks, including in Word, OneNote, Teams and Edge.
why_use_it: It gives a learner reading support — such as larger text, wider spacing, and a read-aloud voice — inside the document or page they're already using, without needing to install anything extra.
how_to: Open the document, OneNote page, or Teams assignment you want to read.
Look for the Immersive Reader icon — a book with an outline of a person — usually on the View tab or in the top-right corner of the screen.
Select the icon to open Immersive Reader.
Select the Play button to have the text read aloud, or use the on-screen controls to change the voice speed.
Select Text Preferences to change text size, spacing or the page's colour theme.
Select Reading Preferences to turn on features such as Line Focus, which highlights one, three or five lines of text at a time.
Close Immersive Reader by selecting the X or back arrow to return to the original document.
